title: Level 541
wikitext:
{{DISPLAYTITLE: }} {{CSS|content= html .resizable-container, .page-content h1, .page-content h2, .page-content h3, .page-content h4, .page-content h5, .page-content h6 { font-family:Bacasime+Antique}} {{CSS|content= @import url('https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Bacasime+Antique&display=swap'); }}
Level 541:
Thawed her mind away!


[[File:Act 1.jpg|link=|center|700x700px]]

{{Collapse|num=1|initial=
Act I: In Between Blue and Blue
|final=
Act I: In Between Blue and Blue
|body=

Took a breather… looked upon the infinite blue and thawed her mind away! Danced nonchalantly once amongst stars. Wished for beauty, for perfection. Gratefully embraced pain, which lingered far away, deep in corners of the mind. As she sought comfort in pain, like a bug that flies toward the light. By the seashore, she drowned among the waves, drowned among the tides. Surrounded in total ataraxy, akin to mesmerizing hope. A heaven reaching for the light that vanished long ago, that which lured all around. Breathing to the pace of the sky, looking at the perpetual beach. A never-ending Eden, a safe home, there to aid the lost, unforgiven souls. Touched by such beauty, such as the timid cannot handle. Her smile exploded in thousands. Her eyes bent over and on. Her face blended with the water. As the millennial suns mocked her, she could only smile; she was in pain, but she was not; she lost herself, but she did not; she found herself, although it was not her. Remodeled, she could now merge with the bay. Like the shells of the seashore: a forgotten corpse, the remains of a life that wanders in unison. As she took a breather, and looked upon the infinite blue: her mind was molten away! Danced nonchalantly, to a song she could not hear.

[[File:Act 2.jpg|link=|center|700x700px]]

{{Collapse|num=2|initial=
Act II: Breath Unauthorized
|final=
Act II: Breath Unauthorized
|body=

The palms waved to the rhythm of the wind. A soft breeze that burned the skin, saltier than regret, colder than ire, a wind that wounded the soul. The sea, sterile, slaughtered by petroleum. An attempt at cleansing, harmed beyond repair. Toxic as possible, engraved by the dead. A pool barking at the incautious. The sand, mauled shells and bones, hope falls in the sinking. Sinking bodies, sinking horror, mauled flesh and hopes. As the suns burn the skin, punish the cleansed, and slowly absorb the remainder of impulse; a life stolen by that which cannot get through. A hellscape forged by the burlesquing, waiting to see us perish by the seashore. Waiting for us to come and enjoy, waiting. Memories slowly fading into the sea . Fading. Fading as the sands swallow, fading as the sea drowns, fading as the sun burns, fading as the wind blows, fading as the mind forgets. Fading into the bay, into the sea, into the sky, into the esoteric. Into the liminal. Erosion. Thoughts. Cranium.

[[File:Act 3.jpg|link=|center|700x700px]]

{{Collapse|num=3|initial=
Act III: Could Not Handle It
|final=
Act III: Could Not Handle It
|body=

She looked upon the infinite blue; Beautiful. Perfect. She felt how a thousand hands explored her, investigated her, in a divine spectacle. She felt herself being dismembered, albeit it was not painful; she welcomed it. Embraced with care, delicate, coordinated. Synchronized to perfection, its fingers moved in and out wherever they could reach. All in a grand spectacle. A beautiful, unique spectacle. Indigo in her veins, fused with the scarlet, the vivid red. Discovered by something new, they explored, as much as the other let them. A strange sensation: she would not be able to explain. Hands gently sectioning her, to be able to melt her, to be able to amalgamate with the being. And she fell from blue to indigo. From the sky to the sea. From the air to the water. Looking upon the infinite blue. Lost was left her mind! An excessively exciting beauty. Such as she could not handle it. Exploded by the bay, although her soul did not die. The blue forgave her. It forgave her. But not her body. Her body was impure, used. It had to be replaced. It would not allow it. She was its. She belonged to it. And it would not pardon her. It would not. It searched for another body.

[[File:Act 4.jpg|link=|center|700x700px]]

{{Collapse|num=4|initial=
Act IV: Roots
|final=
Act IV: Roots
|body=

As he entered the bay, with broken arms and hopes, finding such a paradise ignited his joy; his hope, his belief, in which, some day eventually, he would escape this hellscape. He would be freed from this torment. We would be freed from this torment. As he entered the bay, with a broken leg and skull, finding such a hell destroyed his hopes; as the wind punished his skin, and the waters drove him mad, as if, fallen down, an angel offered to help the man. To help her. As he entered the waters, judged by the fallen sapient, sinking into the petroleum, his body felt numb. His legs, nonexistent. His body, nonexistent. He, nonexistent. He should proceed, the angel said. As he sank in the waters, judged by the sea, his body began to hurt; as if pierced by a thousand knives, as if mauled by a dozen saws. But the wounds were nonexistent. “He should sink,” the demon said. As his face melted by the water, with nothing but dread and blood, his eyes approximated the sea. But he could not close them. He could not move; it would not move him. Almost there, his eyes only could look above and below. As he entered the bay, he should have retreated, gone as far as he could. As they saw him plead, only it would smile. “Why would it do that?” she asked to herself, as his eyes almost sank.

[[File:Act 5.jpg|link=|center|700x700px]]

{{Collapse|num=5|initial=
Act V: Eyes in the Water
|final=
Act V: Eyes in the Water
|body=

His eyes touched the water. He felt how his sockets were flooded with salt and combustion. But he could not close them. His eyes were in the water. He chose to follow its sayings. He was lured by a demon, not an angel. As he believed he had found a safe haven, he let his guard down. Eyes in the water. Unstoppable, unbearable. Such acidity; his eyes dried out. Could not cry, could not bear it. He could have avoided it. Could he? Eyes in the water. He could not close them, it was making. He could not stop it, it was making. He could not avoid it. Eyes in the water. The sea was thick, unsurvivable, unlivable, unbearable. As clock strikings, every second was pain. He could not scream or plead. His fate was decided long ago. Back when his eyes were in the water. Where were his hazel eyes? Where was his radiant smile? Where was his soul? In an eternal purgatory, was the answer. He felt his body being stripped away from him. Eyes in the water. Eyes in the petroleum. Eyes in the sea. Eyes in the purgatory. A body for the demon. A body for her. A body for them. A body for the sky. And a soul for the sea. But the body was broken. Unfit, unusable. She deserved better. But she did not want it. She wanted freedom. It searched for another body.

[[File:Act 6.jpg|link=|center|700x700px]]

{{Collapse|num=6|initial=
Act VI: The Demon of the Daughter’s Bay
|final=
Act VI: The Demon of the Daughter’s Bay
|body=

It lures its prey and strips their bodies away. Searching for a body, a soul in a cage. A loop of torment and rain, in which she finds herself. A lamenter of a bay, a daughter in pain. The demon of the bay searches for a body. Not for himself, but for her, which was taken away. It was used, he proclaims, but why? Trying to gladden only hurt her beyond belief. Why does he kill, why does he slay? What have these innocent people done to be flayed? In the sinking water, their bodies are stripped. In a mock of disgust, she denies to wear a corpse. Eyeless they are. They cannot see it. They cannot see her. She cannot see them. But it can. As days passed on and on, the bay became a grave. A mausoleum to the souls in eternal darkness. An emotional cacophony, a routine of manslaughter. She did not want this anymore. But she was scared. Terrified. What would the demon do to her? What atrocities would it commit? She remained silent. Was that her fault? It was. Was it not? It was her. It was her fault. The deaths. The screams. The pleas. She felt how a thousand tears judged her. How a thousand eyes twitched in suffering. Did the demon know that? She wondered. She dreamt. She dreamt she had not come, she had not had to be aware. She dreamt. The dreaming made her think. The thinking made her feel. The feeling made her act. The acting made her ready. Ready to talk to the demon.

[[File:Act 7.jpg|link=|center|700x700px]]

{{Collapse|num=7|initial=
Act VII: A Scarlet Tear
|final=
Act VII: A Scarlet Tear
|body=

She confronted it. By the seashore, the shells listened. “Why?” It remained silent. It did not growl, attack, or lunge. It did not respond. “Why did you do that?” Silence. It seemed sorry. Or perhaps it masqueraded its desire to kill. It was untellable. A poker face that showed regret. The eyes of the heavens watched it all. The dawn breeze was as silent as never. The suns of the millennia were muted. The petroleum contemplated a snapping couple. Why? The demon thought he had done it right. The demon thought she wanted to be perfect. Delightful. Beautiful. They did not know. “You are a monster!” The demon was shocked. A poker face. Why was it a monster? What had it done wrong? It had only done what it believed she desired. She snapped. “Do not kill anyone else! Monster! Slayer! Killer!” It shed a scarlet tear. Then another. And another. and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another and another. She saw it all clearly. It was not a demon. It was a lone sapient. With a mind like yours and mine. A mind he opened to share. A tear shed from unawareness. From eyes, eyes which screamed pain, eyes which screamed regret. By the bay, they reconciled. “I just want freedom,” It feared it could not give it.

[[File:Act 8.jpg|link=|center|700x700px]]

{{Collapse|num=8|initial=
Act VIII: Laughed in Binary
|final=
Act VIII: Laughed in Binary
|body=

They sat by the shore. Looked upon the petroleum sea. Eyes lost in the sky. Eyes lost in the vastness. It could not give freedom. It could not give escape. The level was his prison. And now hers. Nonchalantly they sat by the palms. Gratefully embraced pain, that which lingered deep in the corners of the mind. Memories slowly fading into the sea. A strange sensation: she would not be able to explain. Finding such a paradise ignited her joy. A body for the sky, a soul for the sea. She dreamt. The demon thought he had done it right… He had. They enjoyed themselves. A paradise that never forgets. Even the never-ending souls did not disturb her. She felt them freed. Freed from what kept them from leaving. The dread, the fear. One might accidentally stumble upon the paradise. Not advised, unpredictable. Upon entrance… They will see an Eden: palms waving to the rhythm of the breeze, a clear sea fixed by hope, delightful sands honoring your presence, and millennial suns bathing one with life. Far in the distance, someone might see a couple contemplating the wonders of the beach. Talking happily. Singing to the heavens. Laughing in binary. Enjoying grace. Took a breather, looked upon the infinite blue… and thawed her mind away!

[[File:Screenshot 2025-08-08 170858-act9?.png|link=|center|700x700px]]
